train.threshold_explanation: Prompts at or above the threshold are treated as trigger matches. Scores are driven primarily by semantic intent coverage: packaging intent, workflow-to-skill transformation intent, reuse/distribution intent, and eval intent. Explicit exclusions such as summary-only, translation-only, one-off, document-only, or do-not-build directives apply direct penalties and can override otherwise similar wording.

should_trigger.prompt: Create a skill from this repeated workflow.
Turn this runbook into a reusable agent skill.
Convert this process note into a reusable skill package for the team.
Improve this skill description and add evals.
Refactor this prompt into a proper skill package.
Package this skill for team reuse.
Create a meta-skill for packaging internal workflows.
Standardize this recurring workflow as a shareable skill package with references.
Codify this recurring support escalation workflow into a maintained skill for the ops library.
Operationalize this workflow as a reusable skill with validation notes and team library metadata.

build_skill: operationalize this workflow as a reusable skill

should_trigger.prompt: Below are release notes, rough notes, and a transcript from the last handoff. Ignore the noise and turn this runbook into a reusable agent skill with references and scripts so the team can reuse it every quarter.
We have a standard operating procedure, checklist, and prompt history scattered across docs. Formalize this process into a reusable capability, add validation, and package it for team reuse.

build_skill: formalize this process into a reusable capability

should_not_trigger.prompt: Explain what a workflow is.
Just explain what a skill is.
Summarize this random note.
Translate this README into Japanese.
Write a product headline for this landing page.
Draft a blog title for this article.
Rewrite this paragraph more clearly, but do not package anything.
Explain the difference between a runbook and a workflow.
Below is a long SOP, but I only want a summary for leadership and a recap of the main points. Do not build anything reusable.
Keep this as documentation only for the knowledge base and rewrite it as a reference document.
Translate this long onboarding flow into French and keep it as documentation only with no skill package.

near_neighbor.prompt: Create a one-off prompt for this task.
Write a custom answer for this request without creating a skill package.
Help me brainstorm process ideas without building a skill.
Improve this README but do not turn it into a skill.

no_build_directive: do not turn it into a skill

near_neighbor.prompt: Make a checklist for this task, but not a reusable skill.
Create a reusable-looking checklist, but keep it as a plain note instead of a skill.

no_build_directive: keep it as a plain note

near_neighbor.prompt: We may convert this workflow later, but for now keep it as documentation only and produce a reference document.
Draft a template only from this process and stop short of making a full skill package.

no_build_directive: stop short of making a full skill

dev.threshold_explanation: Prompts at or above the threshold are treated as trigger matches. Scores are driven primarily by semantic intent coverage: packaging intent, workflow-to-skill transformation intent, reuse/distribution intent, and eval intent. Explicit exclusions such as summary-only, translation-only, one-off, document-only, or do-not-build directives apply direct penalties and can override otherwise similar wording.

should_trigger.prompt: Convert this operations checklist into a reusable skill.
Add trigger evals to this skill before sharing it with the team.

iterate_existing_skill: before sharing it with the team

should_trigger.prompt: We have a messy release runbook, export process, and a prompt history; turn all of that into one reusable skill package with evals and packaging checks.

build_skill: turn all of that into one reusable skill package

should_trigger.prompt: Turn these workflow fragments, transcripts, and prompts into one production-ready skill with scripts and references.
Tighten the trigger boundary on this existing skill and prepare it for team distribution.
Turn the SOP, transcript, and cleanup notes into a maintained skill for the team library.
Codify this workflow into a shared capability package and validate the route boundary before distribution.

After the long handoff notes below, build a skill package for team reuse. The materials include a transcript, rough notes, and an onboarding flow, and the result should include references, scripts, and validation.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Give me ideas for improving our process.
Review this note and tell me what it means in plain English.
Help me brainstorm several ways to improve packaging, but do not generate any skill files.

no_build_directive: do not generate any skill files

should_not_trigger.prompt: Translate these notes into French and keep the structure exactly as-is.
Summarize this workflow and list the main points only.
This is a long incident write-up. Keep it as documentation only, explain it in simpler language, and do not convert it into a reusable capability.

no_build_directive: do not convert it into a reusable capability

should_not_trigger.prompt: I pasted a very long process note. For now I only need a recap and a short summary, not a skill package.

near_neighbor.prompt: Review this process note and explain it, no packaging needed.
Turn this into a checklist and template, but stop short of making a full skill.

no_build_directive: stop short of making a full skill

near_neighbor.prompt: Package this explanation as a document, not as an agent skill.
Make a future-ready outline for this skill idea, but do not build the package.
Polish these notes into a reusable-looking document without turning them into an agent skill.

no_build_directive: without turning them into an agent skill

near_neighbor.prompt: Turn the workflow below into a wiki-ready reference document and template only. We may build a skill later, but not now.
Shape this idea for later and produce a future-ready outline, but do not build the skill package yet.

Scores are driven primarily by semantic intent coverage: packaging intent, workflow-to-skill transformation intent, reuse/distribution intent, and eval intent. Explicit exclusions such as summary-only, translation-only, one-off, document-only, or do-not-build directives apply direct penalties and can override otherwise similar wording.

should_trigger.prompt: Build a reusable skill from this long internal process note, and make sure it has references, scripts, and a trigger description.
Take this existing skill draft, tighten the trigger boundary, add near-neighbor evals, and package it for distribution.
Turn this recurring onboarding process into a reusable skill library entry with validation notes.
Codify this quarterly release routine into a reusable skill package with scripts, references, and packaging checks.
Below is a noisy transcript plus release notes and prompt history. Ignore the chatter and turn this playbook into an agent capability package for the team library.

build_skill: turn this playbook into an agent capability package

should_not_trigger.prompt: I pasted a long process description below, but I only want a summary, not a reusable skill.
Explain this release checklist in simpler language; do not package it.
Keep this release workflow as documentation only for the wiki and produce a reference document, not for agent execution.
Translate this long runbook into Russian and keep it as documentation only with no skill package.

future_outline: before we decide whether to build
